title: Alpha Diversity Pipeline — Parameters
description: Nextflow schema for the alpha diversity analysis pipeline.

input_output_options.title: Input / Output Options
input_output_options.description: Define input files and output destinations.

feature_table.description: Path to the feature table. Accepted formats: BIOM (.biom), TSV, or GTDB TSV.
feature_table.help_text: When input_format is 'biom' this must be a BIOM file. For 'tsv' or 'gtdb' this must be a tab-separated file with feature IDs as row names and sample IDs as column headers (or vice-versa — orientation is auto-detected).

meta_table.description: Path to sample metadata CSV. First column must contain sample IDs that match those in the feature table.
meta_table.help_text: Rows are samples, columns are metadata variables. The first column is used as row names.

taxonomy_table.description: Path to a two-column TSV: col 1 = feature ID, col 2 = semicolon-separated taxonomy string.
taxonomy_table.help_text: Required when input_format is 'tsv' or 'gtdb'. Ignored when input_format is 'biom' (taxonomy is embedded). Supports SILVA (D_0__, D_1__, …) and GTDB (d__, p__, …) prefixes.

tree_file.description: Optional phylogenetic tree file. Accepted for API consistency but not used in alpha diversity calculations.
tree_file.help_text: Provide a Newick-format tree if required by downstream workflows. Has no effect on current outputs.

input_format.description: Format of the input feature table.
input_format.help_text: biom: phyloseq import_biom(); tsv: tab-separated with SILVA-style taxonomy; gtdb: tab-separated with GTDB-style taxonomy prefixes.

output_dir.description: Directory where output CSV files are written.

filtering_options.title: Filtering Options
filtering_options.description: Control sample and feature filtering applied before analysis.

min_library_size.description: Minimum total read count per sample. Samples below this threshold are dropped.
min_library_size.help_text: Set lower for low-depth datasets (e.g. 1000) or higher for strict quality control.

exclude_column.description: Metadata column used to exclude samples. Pairs with exclude_values.
exclude_column.help_text: Leave empty to skip sample exclusion.

exclude_values.description: Comma-separated values in exclude_column that identify samples to remove.
exclude_values.help_text: Example: 'control,blank'. Only applied when exclude_column is also set.

grouping_options.title: Grouping Options
grouping_options.description: Define how samples are grouped for statistical comparisons and output annotation.

group.description: One metadata column, or comma-separated columns to paste together as the group label.

type.description: One metadata column, or comma-separated columns to paste together as the point-style label (optional).

alpha_diversity_options.title: Alpha Diversity Options
alpha_diversity_options.description: Control taxonomy collation, metric selection, and statistical testing.

taxon_rank.description: Taxonomic level at which features are collapsed before diversity calculation.
taxon_rank.help_text: Use 'Feature' to work at the feature level with no collation.

label.description: Label appended to output filenames. Must be a valid filename component.

alpha_metrics.description: Comma-separated list of alpha diversity metrics to compute.
alpha_metrics.help_text: Available metrics: Richness (rarefied species richness via vegan::rarefy), Shannon (vegan::diversity index='shannon'), Simpson (vegan::diversity index='simpson'), InvSimpson (vegan::diversity index='invsimpson'), FisherAlpha (vegan::fisher.alpha), Chao1 (vegan::estimateR S.chao1), PielouEvenness (Shannon / ln(S)). Example: 'Shannon,Chao1,PielouEvenness'.

test_method.description: Statistical test used for pairwise group comparisons.
test_method.help_text: anova: one-way ANOVA (or within-subjects ANOVA when connections_column is set). kruskal: Kruskal-Wallis rank-sum test (non-parametric). none: skip statistical testing and write an empty pairwise CSV.

p_adjust_method.description: Method for multiple-testing correction of pairwise p-values.
p_adjust_method.help_text: Applied per metric across all pairwise comparisons using R's p.adjust(). BH (Benjamini-Hochberg) and fdr are equivalent. Use 'none' to report unadjusted p-values.

institutional_config_options.title: Institutional Config Options
institutional_config_options.description: Parameters for institutional or HPC-specific configuration.

custom_config_base.description: Base URL or path to nf-core/configs institutional configuration files.

output_options.title: Output Options
output_options.description: Additional output formats produced after all analyses complete.

merge_parquet.description: Merge all output CSVs into a single Parquet file with analysis and table metadata columns.
merge_parquet.help_text: Writes alpha_diversity_{label}.parquet to output_dir. Filter by 'analysis' and 'table' columns downstream.
